message:
feat(render-svg,cli): add sequence diagram fragment rendering and config validation

Implement SVG rendering of sequence diagram fragments (loop, alt, par,
opt, critical, break) as labeled rectangles with proper nesting. The CLI
gains runtime config validation and plumbing for back-edge display and
minimap toggle.

- fm-render-svg: add end-to-end sequence fragment rendering tests
  covering loop, alt, par, nested fragments, opt, critical, break,
  and empty fragments; fragments render as fm-sequence-fragment
  classed rects with fm-sequence-fragment-label text elements
- fm-cli/main.rs: add show_back_edges and show_minimap fields to
  RenderCommandOptions, RenderSurfaceOptions, and
  ValidateCommandOptions; add validate_runtime_config_support() that
  rejects unsupported config combinations (deterministic=false,
  fallback_on_error=false, intent_inference=false) at load time;
  add resolve_show_back_edges helper
- fm-cli/tests/config_roundtrip_test.rs: expand config tests for
  new validation paths
- fm-render-term/config.rs: add show_minimap field
- fm-wasm: minor adjustment for layout config changes
- Add tests/sequence_fragment_test.rs with standalone fragment tests
- Update Cargo.lock and beads issue tracker

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-b0pc.10","title":"Profile and optimize incremental layout path to beat full recompute","description":"Reality check found: The incremental layout path (try_incremental_subgraph_relayout) is currently SLOWER than full recompute for all tested graph sizes. Criterion benchmarks at crates/fm-layout/benches/incremental_layout.rs show: single-node edit on 1000 nodes takes ~7.8ms incremental vs ~3.5ms full recompute.

## Root Cause Analysis (from code review)
The overhead comes from these phases in try_incremental_subgraph_relayout:
1. LayoutDependencyGraph::from_ir() — O(V+E) graph construction even when cached graph is reusable
2. dirty_node_indexes_for_edits() — walks both old and new dependency graphs
3. incremental_region_members() — expands dirty set to include 1-hop neighbors
4. build_subgraph_local_layout() — runs full Sugiyama on subgraph
5. build_edge_paths() — rebuilds ALL edges, not just those touching dirty nodes
6. smooth_boundary_edges() — iterates all edges for boundary detection

## Optimization Plan
1. **Cache dependency graph across calls**: currently if cache key matches, the graph is cloned; switch to Arc sharing to avoid the clone cost
2. **Incremental edge rebuild**: only rebuild edges where at least one endpoint is in the dirty node set — reuse cached edge paths for clean-to-clean edges
3. **Skip overlap alignment for small dirty sets**: if dirty_members.len() <= 3, the overlap alignment cost exceeds the value; use simple centroid anchoring
4. **Lazy dependency graph**: only construct when topology actually changed (edge add/remove); for label-only edits, reuse cached graph without reconstruction
5. **Batch dirty detection**: coalesce multiple edits into a single dirty set instead of iterating per-edit
6. After each optimization: re-run cargo bench --bench incremental_layout and record improvement

## Success Criteria
- Single-node label edit on 500-node graph: incremental >= 2x faster than full recompute
- Single-node label edit on 1000-node graph: incremental >= 3x faster than full recompute
- 5-node cluster edit on 500-node graph: incremental >= 1.5x faster than full recompute
- Small graph bypass (< 50 nodes): zero performance regression from optimization changes
- All existing incremental layout tests still pass (currently 348 fm-layout tests)

## Test Plan
- Before/after Criterion benchmark comparison (cargo bench --bench incremental_layout)
- Run full fm-layout test suite after each optimization (cargo test -p fm-layout)
- Add a specific regression test: large graph single-node edit must complete in < 5ms
- flamegraph profiling before and after to verify hotspot elimination

## Cross-reference
- Benchmark infrastructure already exists at crates/fm-layout/benches/incremental_layout.rs
- Incremental layout implementation at try_incremental_subgraph_relayout (~lines 2814-3012 in lib.rs)
- Related to bd-2u0.7 (incremental pipeline for WASM editing) — performance directly impacts interactive UX","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:39:06.501034614Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T23:07:01.707486303Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["incremental","layout","performance","reality-check"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b0pc.10","depends_on_id":"bd-b0pc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:39:06.501034614Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-b0pc.11","title":"Add end-to-end CLI smoke tests for all 24 diagram types","description":"Reality check found: No single test verifies ALL 24 diagram types through the complete CLI pipeline.

## Test Cases — One per diagram type (24 total)
For each, run detect/parse/render and assert success:
1. flowchart LR; A-->B-->C
2. sequenceDiagram; Alice->>Bob: hello
3. classDiagram; class Animal { +name: string }
4. stateDiagram-v2; [*] --> Active; Active --> [*]
5. erDiagram; CUSTOMER ||--o{ ORDER : places
6. gantt; title Plan; section A; Task1: a1, 2024-01-01, 7d
7. pie title Votes; Dogs: 70; Cats: 30
8. gitGraph; commit; branch dev; commit
9. journey; title Day; section Morning; Wake up: 5: Me
10. mindmap; root((Central)); Branch1; Branch2
11. timeline; title History; 2020: Event A; 2021: Event B
12. sankey-beta; Source,Target,10; Source,Other,5
13. quadrantChart; title Skills; x-axis Low --> High; y-axis Low --> High; A: [0.3, 0.6]
14. xychart-beta; title Sales; x-axis [Q1, Q2, Q3]; line [10, 20, 15]
15. block-beta; columns 2; A; B; C; D
16. packet-beta; 0-15: Source Port; 16-31: Dest Port
17. architecture-beta; group api; service auth(server)[Auth]
18. C4Context; Person(user, User); System(sys, System); Rel(user, sys, Uses)
19. C4Container; Container(app, App); Container(db, DB)
20. C4Component; Component(auth, Auth); Component(api, API)
21. C4Dynamic; Person(user, User); Rel(user, api, Call)
22. C4Deployment; Deployment_Node(server, Server) { Container(app, App) }
23. requirementDiagram; requirement req1 { id: 1; text: Must work }
24. kanban; column Todo; card Task1; card Task2

## Cross-Cutting Feature Tests (beyond diagram types)
25. Init directive: '%%{init: {"theme":"dark"}}%%\nflowchart LR; A-->B' — verify parse succeeds and theme metadata is captured
26. DOT bridge: 'digraph G { A -> B; B -> C }' — verify detected as Flowchart, renders successfully
27. Fuzzy detection: 'flowchrt LR; A-->B' — verify detected as Flowchart with confidence < 1.0
28. Error recovery: 'flowchart LR; A-->B; !!!invalid!!!; C-->D' — verify parse succeeds with warnings, both valid edges present in IR

## Per-test assertions
- detect returns correct DiagramType with confidence >= 0.5
- parse produces IR with at least 1 node or 1 edge
- render --format svg produces output starting with '<svg' or '<?xml'
- render --format term produces non-empty terminal output
- No panics on any input
- Log: diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, svg_bytes, term_chars, parse_ms, layout_ms

## Implementation
- Integration test file: fm-cli/tests/all_types_e2e.rs
- Const array of (input, expected_type, min_nodes) tuples
- Structured JSON logging per test case for CI visibility
- Report total pass/fail count at end

## Cross-reference
- Depends on bd-b0pc.8 (sequence fragment improvements)
- This is the ultimate integration test — 28 test cases covering every diagram type plus cross-cutting features","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:39:06.690596323Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T02:53:54.613396476Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","e2e","reality-check","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b0pc.11","depends_on_id":"bd-b0pc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:39:06.690596323Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-b0pc.11","depends_on_id":"bd-b0pc.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:39:33.225513946Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-b0pc.12","title":"Add config file round-trip test (TOML parse -> apply -> verify effect)","description":"Companion test bead for bd-b0pc.7 (config file wiring). Proves the config system works end-to-end, not just that the file parses.

## Test Cases
1. Theme override test:
   - Write TOML with theme='dark'
   - Run fm-cli render with --config, capture SVG
   - Assert SVG contains dark theme background color (not default blue/white)
   - Repeat with theme='forest', verify different colors

2. Feature toggle test:
   - Write TOML with shadows=false, gradients=false
   - Render SVG, assert NO feDropShadow filter and NO linearGradient/radialGradient elements
   - Then shadows=true, gradients=true — assert both present

3. Accessibility toggle test:
   - Write TOML with accessibility=true
   - Assert SVG contains role='img' and aria-label attributes
   - Write accessibility=false, verify no ARIA attributes

4. Layout config test:
   - Write TOML with node_spacing=100 (double the default 48)
   - Render same diagram with and without config
   - Assert configured version has larger bounding box (wider spacing)

5. Terminal config test:
   - Write TOML with [term] tier='compact'
   - Render to terminal, assert output is shorter/narrower than default

6. Invalid config test:
   - Write malformed TOML (missing quotes, invalid keys)
   - Assert fm-cli exits with non-zero code and prints helpful error message
   - Assert the error message includes the file path and problematic line

7. Config discovery test:
   - Create frankenmermaid.toml in a temp directory
   - Run fm-cli from that directory (no --config flag)
   - Verify the config is automatically discovered and applied

## Implementation
- Integration test file: fm-cli/tests/config_roundtrip_test.rs
- Use tempfile crate for temporary TOML files
- Each test: write TOML → render → parse output → assert config took effect
- Log: config path, parsed config summary, output characteristics

## Cross-reference
- Depends on bd-b0pc.7 (config loading must work first)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:39:06.883968589Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T23:07:42.717068572Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["config","reality-check","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b0pc.12","depends_on_id":"bd-b0pc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:39:06.883968589Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-b0pc.12","depends_on_id":"bd-b0pc.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:39:32.889297316Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-b0pc.13","title":"Update AGENTS.md crate line counts to match current reality","description":"Reality check found: AGENTS.md and README both have crate line-count tables that are significantly stale. README says fm-layout is ~8,400 lines (actually 17,577+), fm-parser as ~8,700 (needs recount). The total of ~37,900 is likely far higher now.

## Scope
1. Run tokei or wc -l on each crate's src/ directory to get accurate line counts:
   - fm-core/src/
   - fm-parser/src/
   - fm-layout/src/
   - fm-render-svg/src/
   - fm-render-term/src/
   - fm-render-canvas/src/
   - fm-wasm/src/
   - fm-cli/src/
2. Update the crate map table in README.md 'Technical Architecture' section
3. Update the 'Key Files by Crate' table in AGENTS.md if present
4. Update the Total line count
5. Round to nearest 100 for readability (e.g., ~17,600 not 17,577)

## Verification
- Sum of individual crate counts should approximately equal Total
- No crate should have a count of 0 or a wildly implausible number
- Counts should be higher than or equal to previous values (code grows, rarely shrinks)

## Cross-reference
- Related to bd-b0pc.1 and bd-b0pc.2 (all edit the same README sections)
- Do this AFTER .1 and .2 to avoid merge conflicts","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:39:24.471983680Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T23:08:02.539326676Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["agents-md","docs","reality-check"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b0pc.13","depends_on_id":"bd-b0pc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:39:24.471983680Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-b0pc.13","depends_on_id":"bd-b0pc.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T23:08:02.538445317Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-b0pc.14","title":"Verify Live Demo URL at dicklesworthstone.github.io/frankenmermaid","description":"Reality check found: README links to https://dicklesworthstone.github.io/frankenmermaid/ as a Live Demo. Investigation reveals web/index.html is a bootstrap loader that fetches a showcase page from an external URL — NOT a self-contained WASM demo.

## Scope
1. Check if the GitHub Pages site loads (curl the URL, check HTTP status)
2. If it loads: check if the bootstrap successfully fetches and renders the showcase
3. If the bootstrap target URL is broken: either fix the source URL or replace with a self-contained WASM demo page
4. If GitHub Pages is not enabled: check repo Settings > Pages configuration
5. Consider: should this be a simple self-contained page that loads the WASM binary from pkg/ and renders a sample diagram? That would be more reliable than a bootstrap loader that depends on an external URL.

## Verification
- curl -sL the URL returns HTTP 200
- The page renders at least one diagram in a browser (manual check or Playwright test)
- No JavaScript console errors on load

## Note
web/index.html is a bootstrap loader (fetch + document.write pattern), NOT a WASM integration. The README's 'Live Demo' implies an interactive demo — verify the bootstrap target delivers this, or replace with a real demo.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:39:24.684024579Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T02:53:19.908188796Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","docs","reality-check","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b0pc.14","depends_on_id":"bd-b0pc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:39:24.684024579Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-b0pc.5","title":"Verify cargo install frankenmermaid path or update README","description":"Reality check found: README says 'cargo install frankenmermaid' under Installation but the crate is not published to crates.io.

## Scope
1. Check if 'frankenmermaid' crate exists on crates.io (curl https://crates.io/api/v1/crates/frankenmermaid)
2. If NOT published:
   a. Option A (preferred if close to release): Prepare Cargo.toml metadata for publishing — all workspace crates need: description, license, repository, categories, keywords, readme
   b. Option B (if not ready): Update README to remove 'cargo install frankenmermaid' section and only show 'From Source' install method, with a note that crates.io publishing is planned
3. If published but outdated: update or add note about version
4. Verify the binary name would be 'fm-cli' (check [[bin]] section in workspace Cargo.toml)
5. Verify 'cargo install frankenmermaid' actually installs the binary correctly (if published)

## Decision Criteria
- If more than 3 workspace crates have missing metadata: go with Option B (update README) now, create separate bead for crates.io publishing prep
- If metadata is mostly complete: go with Option A

## Verification
- After fix: README installation section only references working install methods
- No broken commands in the Installation section

## Cross-reference
- Related to bd-6qd.3 (CLI binary distribution) and bd-6qd.5 (release canary)
- This is about the README claim, not the full publishing workflow","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:38:13.300696615Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T02:33:38.074959434Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T02:33:38.074665588Z","close_reason":"Completed: crates.io API for frankenmermaid returns 404, fm-cli is the actual binary, and more than three workspace crates are missing publish metadata; README installation now advertises only working install paths and notes crates.io publishing is planned.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crates-io","distribution","reality-check"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b0pc.5","depends_on_id":"bd-b0pc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:38:13.300696615Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-b0pc.6","title":"Regenerate TypeScript definitions to include missing WASM exports","description":"Reality check found: pkg/frankenmermaid.d.ts is missing TypeScript definitions for 3 exported WASM functions: diagramLens, applyLensEdit, describeDiagram. These ARE implemented in Rust and exported via wasm_bindgen, but the .d.ts was generated before they were added.

## Scope
1. Re-run wasm-pack build (via build-wasm.sh) to regenerate .d.ts
2. Verify all 10 functions appear in the new .d.ts: init, renderSvg, detectType, parse, sourceSpans, diagramLens, applyLensEdit, describeDiagram, capabilityMatrix, render
3. Verify the Diagram class and all its methods appear: constructor, render, setTheme, on, destroy
4. Verify function signatures have correct parameter types (not just 'any' for everything)
5. Commit the updated .d.ts, .js glue, and .wasm binary
6. If build-wasm.sh fails, fix the build script (may need wasm-pack/wasm-opt installed)

## Test Plan
- diff old .d.ts vs new .d.ts — should show 3+ new export lines
- TypeScript compiler check: write a small .ts file that imports all 10 functions and the Diagram class — verify it compiles with tsc --noEmit
- Count exports: grep -c 'export function\|export class' pkg/frankenmermaid.d.ts should be >= 11

## Cross-reference
- Depends on bd-b0pc.3 (npm package name fix) — want correct name in generated defs
- Requires wasm-pack toolchain (may need to be installed on build machine)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:38:13.486380153Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T03:24:13.064393496Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T03:24:13.064009790Z","close_reason":"Added wasm_bindgen exports for diagramLens, applyLensEdit, describeDiagram. Regenerated .d.ts and .wasm via build-wasm.sh. All 11 exports now present (1 class + 10 functions). WASM still within 500KB gzip budget (432KB).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["reality-check","typescript","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b0pc.6","depends_on_id":"bd-b0pc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:38:13.486380153Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-b0pc.6","depends_on_id":"bd-b0pc.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:39:33.053329066Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-b0pc.7","title":"Verify and wire up frankenmermaid.toml config file loading in CLI","description":"CRITICAL GAP: README documents '--config <path>' as a Global Flag (line 253), but the actual clap Cli struct in main.rs has NO --config argument. The config file feature is entirely unimplemented in the CLI. The README also shows a detailed frankenmermaid.toml format with [core], [parser], [layout], [render], [svg], [term] sections.

## Scope (implementation required, not just verification)
1. Add --config <path> global argument to the Cli clap struct in main.rs
2. Add config file auto-discovery: check ./frankenmermaid.toml, then ~/.config/frankenmermaid/config.toml
3. Define a FrankenmermaidConfig struct matching the documented TOML schema:
   - [core]: deterministic, max_input_bytes, fallback_on_error
   - [parser]: intent_inference, fuzzy_keyword_distance, auto_close_delimiters, create_placeholder_nodes
   - [layout]: algorithm, cycle_strategy, node_spacing, rank_spacing, edge_routing
   - [render]: default_format, show_back_edges, reduced_motion
   - [svg]: theme, rounded_corners, shadows, gradients, accessibility
   - [term]: tier, unicode, minimap
4. Parse TOML config file using toml crate (add as dependency if not present)
5. Merge config into existing SvgRenderConfig, TermRenderConfig, LayoutConfig structs
6. CLI flags override config file values (flags take precedence)
7. Add clear error messages for: file not found, invalid TOML, unknown keys
8. Log which config file was loaded (at -v verbosity level)

## Test Plan
- Unit test: parse sample TOML into FrankenmermaidConfig struct
- Unit test: merge config with CLI defaults (config overrides defaults, CLI flags override config)
- Integration test: write temp TOML with theme=dark, run fm-cli render --config <file>, verify dark SVG
- Negative test: --config with non-existent file produces clear error with path
- Negative test: malformed TOML produces error with line number
- Discovery test: create frankenmermaid.toml in temp dir, run fm-cli from that dir, verify auto-loaded
- Precedence test: config says theme=dark, CLI says --theme forest, verify forest wins

## Cross-reference
- bd-b0pc.12 depends on this (config round-trip tests)
- Related to bd-6qd.4 (versioned config schema) — this is runtime loading, that's schema formalization
- toml crate may need to be added to fm-cli Cargo.toml dependencies","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:38:40.477899944Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T02:58:00.984817349Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","config","reality-check"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b0pc.7","depends_on_id":"bd-b0pc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:38:40.477899944Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-b0pc.8","title":"Improve sequence diagram fragment rendering (loop/alt/par nesting)","description":"Reality check found: Sequence diagrams are marked 'Partial' in the README. The parser comprehensively captures fragments (loop, alt, par, opt, neg), boxes, notes, activations, and 26 arrow types. But layout/rendering of fragment nesting may be incomplete.

## Scope
1. Create comprehensive test inputs with nested fragments:
   a. Simple fragment: 'loop Every minute; A->>B: ping; end'
   b. Nested alt/else: 'alt success; A->>B: ok; else failure; A->>B: err; end'
   c. Double-nested: 'loop; alt; A->>B: yes; else; A->>B: no; end; end'
   d. Par blocks: 'par; A->>B: one; and; A->>C: two; end'
   e. Fragment with box: 'box Group; participant A; participant B; end'
2. Render each to SVG and terminal, inspect output:
   a. Are fragment boundaries visually rendered (labeled rectangles)?
   b. Are nested fragments properly indented/contained?
   c. Do fragment labels (loop, alt, else, par) appear in the output?
   d. Are activation bars rendered on lifelines?
3. If fragments are NOT visually rendered:
   a. Add fragment boundary rendering to fm-render-svg: draw labeled rounded-rect for each fragment region
   b. Add fragment support to fm-render-term: draw box chars around fragment regions
   c. The LayoutExtensions.sequence_fragments (or similar) field should carry fragment geometry
4. If fragments ARE rendered but poorly:
   a. Fix nesting overlap issues
   b. Ensure label positioning doesn't collide with messages
5. Once complete: update README status from 'Partial' to 'Implemented'

## Test Plan
- Golden SVG test for each of the 5 fragment test cases above
- Assert SVG output contains 'rect' elements for fragment boundaries (not just messages)
- Assert terminal output has box-drawing characters around fragment regions
- Property test: any valid sequence input with fragments produces SVG with fragment-count rect elements

## Cross-reference
- bd-b0pc.11 (e2e smoke tests) depends on this
- No existing bead covers sequence fragment rendering specifically","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:38:40.665916008Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T03:24:52.220526921Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["layout","reality-check","render","sequence"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b0pc.8","depends_on_id":"bd-b0pc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:38:40.665916008Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-b0pc.9","title":"Add PNG rasterization smoke test under --features png","description":"Reality check found: README documents PNG output via '--features png' using resvg/usvg, but there are zero tests for this feature in the CI suite.

## Scope
1. Verify the png feature compiles: cargo check -p fm-cli --features png
2. If it fails: fix Cargo.toml dependency versions for resvg/usvg, update any API changes
3. Create integration test in fm-cli/tests/ (feature-gated with #[cfg(feature = "png")]):
   a. Render 'flowchart LR; A-->B-->C' to PNG via the render pipeline
   b. Verify output file exists and is non-empty
   c. Check PNG magic bytes: first 8 bytes should be [0x89, 0x50, 0x4E, 0x47, 0x0D, 0x0A, 0x1A, 0x0A]
   d. Verify image dimensions are reasonable (> 100x100 pixels, < 10000x10000)
   e. Test with different themes: default and dark should produce different PNG content
4. Add CI job or test configuration that runs with --features png
5. If resvg/usvg are too heavy for CI, add a note about optional PNG support

## Test Plan
- cargo test -p fm-cli --features png -- png (run only PNG-specific tests)
- Assert PNG magic bytes
- Assert file size > 1KB (not empty/trivial)
- Assert multiple diagram types produce valid PNGs (flowchart + sequence at minimum)
- Log file sizes and generation times for performance baseline

## Cross-reference
- PNG feature is documented in README under 'fm-cli render' and under 'Feature Flags'
- No existing bead covers PNG testing","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:38:40.852743651Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T23:06:39.696179011Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["png","reality-check","render","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b0pc.9","depends_on_id":"bd-b0pc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:38:40.852743651Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-b6gg","title":"Implement accDescription and accTitle accessibility directives","description":"# Accessibility Directives

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
accTitle: Process Flow
accDescr: This diagram shows the order processing pipeline from receipt to delivery.
accDescr {
  Multi-line description
  of the diagram's purpose
}
\`\`\`
- accTitle: sets the accessible title for screen readers (maps to SVG <title> element)
- accDescr: sets the accessible description (maps to SVG <desc> element)
- Both are global, apply to the entire diagram
- Used by all diagram types

## Current State
- accTitle/accDescr are NOT parsed
- SVG renderer already has accessibility support (a11y) but doesn't read these from IR

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Recognize accTitle and accDescr (single-line and multi-line block) directives. Store in MermaidDiagramMeta.
2. **IR**: Add accessible_title: Option<String> and accessible_description: Option<String> to MermaidDiagramMeta.
3. **SVG Renderer**: Emit <title> and <desc> elements within the root <svg> element.
4. **Tests**: Single-line accTitle, accDescr, multi-line accDescr block, SVG output verification.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:32:12.842946048Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-24T05:17:20.129238031Z","closed_at":"2026-03-24T05:17:20.128753604Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["a11y","parity","parser","render"],"comments":[{"id":174,"issue_id":"bd-b6gg","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:32:22Z"},{"id":194,"issue_id":"bd-b6gg","author":"ubuntu","text":"SPECIFIC TEST CASES:
1. accTitle: 'Process Flow' → verify <title> element in SVG root
2. accDescr: 'Single line description' → verify <desc> element in SVG root
3. accDescr { multi-line } → verify multi-line <desc>
4. Both accTitle and accDescr → verify both elements present
5. Neither present → verify no empty <title>/<desc> elements
6. Works with flowchart, sequence, class, state → verify across diagram types","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:24Z"}]}
